DEMURRER TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE AN ACTUAL PRESENT CONTROVERSY FOR WHICH A DECLARATION IS SOUGHT. DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUL-30-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS KRISTIN WONG, CHRISTIAN WONG, AND KALEEN WONG'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR FEES, COSTS AND MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF CHIKODI CHIMA AND HIS ATTORNEY OF RECORD, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS KRISTIN WONG, CHRISTIAN WONG, AND KALEEN WONG MOTION TO COMPEL AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS ARE GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) DEFENSE COUNSEL SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM WHICH SHOULD RECITE THE COURT'S RULING BUT WHICH MAY ALSO ADD THE SPECIFIC DISCOVERY REQUEST NUMBERS THAT WERE THE SUBJECT OF DEFENDANTS' MOTION. Off Calendar Dec-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via CourtCall:

Davis J. Reilly, Esq., (415) 981-5411, appearing for Kristin Wong and Christian
Wong, Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court stated that it concurred with the pro temp. Mr. Reily stated that he contested the tentative ruling, provided case background and requested that this motion be denied without prejudice.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the modified tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KRISTIN WONG, CHRISTIAN WONG MOTION TO STRIKE is DENIED with prejudice. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: N. Wong, Court reporter: Raquel Sharp. CSR #10619. Telephone (925)384-0003 . (D501)

Amended DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: (Court Call)
Karen Uchiyama, 415.563.9300 for Plaintiff Chikodi Chima.
Davis Reilly, 415.981.5411 for Defendants Kristin Wong and Christian Wong.

Mr. Reilly informs the Court that he is counsel of record for the Defendants and specially appearing for the defendants as cross-complainants. The Court orders Mr. Reilly to file a limited scope appearance for the cross-complainants by the end of business Sep-08-20.

The Court strikes Ms. Uchiyama's late reply.

Hearing Held. Argued by Karen Uchiyama. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT CHIKODI CHIMA Amended DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of contract is OVERRULED. Cross-Complaint raises issues of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. Demurrer to the cause of action for declaratory relief is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege an actual present controversy for which a declaration is sought. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Atty. DAVIS J. REILLY, Esq. of Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane (Tel: 415-981-5411) for defendants Kristin F. Wong, Christian Tse-Hung Wong, and Kaleen Wong;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS KRISTIN WONG, CHRISTIAN WONG, AND KALEEN WONG'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses, Production Of Documents And Request For Fees, Costs And Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Chikodi Chima And His Attorney Of Record.

Defense counsel stipulates to the matter being decided by the judge pro tem. The judge pro tem and defense counsel both appearing remotely authorize the clerk to sign the stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants KRISTIN WONG, CHRISTIAN WONG, and KALEEN WONG Motion to Compel and Request for sanctions are GRANTED. Plaintiff shall produce all responsive documents, including any photographs in their native format not previously produced, within 14 calendar days of this hearing. Defendants are awarded sanctions (which were unopposed) in the amount of $5,644.50 for Plaintiff's misuse of the discovery process including untimely responses which waived objections (which was not disputed), maintaining those and other improper objections, failing to provide a privilege log despite asserting objections on privilege grounds; and failing to provide full and complete discovery responses.

Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the judge pro tem which should recite the court's ruling but which may also add the specific discovery request numbers that were the subject of defendants' motion.

Judge Pro Tem: Rebecca Grey; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
